name: Integration Criterion
definition: A constraint is integrated iff the system cannot revert to prior behavior under equivalent structural conditions.

instability_signature.description: Canonical pattern representing the prior instability that must not reoccur

failure_condition: Reproduction of prior behavior under equivalent conditions

non_reversion_scope.description: Number of repeated validations under equivalent structural conditions required to confirm non-reversion

constraint_preservation_check.description: Ensures no existing constraints are removed, weakened, or bypassed to achieve pass condition

condition_equivalence_requirements: Input structure must match prior failure context
Relevant system state variables must be within tolerance bounds
No structural constraints removed or bypassed to achieve pass condition

verification_mode.description: replay = re-execution under original conditions; simulation = modeled re-execution; live = real-time observation

edge_constraints: Equivalent structural conditions must be explicitly defined
Resolution that introduces new constraints must be flagged separately
implications: Integration is behaviorally verifiable, not epistemically claimed
Distinguishes true resolution from deferral or displacement
notes: Core invariant for all downstream validation, including motif stability, governance integrity, and trajectory inference. Resolution classification: resolution = instability eliminated; suppression = instability hidden but recoverable; displacement = instability moved to another subsystem.
